heading: Clean fuel production credit
label: 45Z Clean fuel production credit

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–169, title I, § 13704(c)136 Stat. 2003Aug. 16, 2022“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 25C, 30C, 38, and 4101 of this title] shall apply to transportation fuel produced after .”December 31, 2024

children.label: i
children.text: produced by the taxpayer at a qualified facility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: sold by the taxpayer in a manner described in paragraph (4) during the taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the emissions factor for such fuel (as determined under subsection (b)).

children.heading: Base amount
children.label: A Base amount
children.text: In the case of any transportation fuel produced at a qualified facility which does not satisfy the requirements described in subparagraph (B), the applicable amount shall be 20 cents.

children.heading: Alternative amount
children.label: B Alternative amount
children.text: In the case of any transportation fuel produced at a qualified facility which satisfies the requirements under paragraphs (6) and (7) of subsection (f), the applicable amount shall be $1.00.

children.label: i
children.text: ASTM International Standard D7566, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Fischer Tropsch provisions of ASTM International Standard D1655, Annex A1, and

children.label: B
children.text: is not derived from palm fatty acid distillates or petroleum.

children.label: A
children.text: for use by such person in the production of a fuel mixture,

children.label: B
children.text: for use by such person in a trade or business, or

children.label: C
children.text: who sells such fuel at retail to another person and places such fuel in the fuel tank of such other person.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 5 Rounding
children.text: If any amount determined under paragraph (1) is not a multiple of 1 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest cent.

children.label: aa
children.text: 50 kilograms of COe per mmBTU, minus2

children.label: bb
children.text: the emissions rate for such fuel, divided by

children.label: II
children.text: 50 kilograms of COe per mmBTU.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clauses (ii), (iii), (iv), and (v), the Secretary shall annually publish a table which sets forth the emissions rate for similar types and categories of transportation fuels based on the amount of lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions (as described in section 211()(1)(H) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)(H)), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section) for such fuels, expressed as kilograms of COe per mmBTU, which a taxpayer shall use for purposes of this section.oo42 U.S.C. 75452

children.heading: Non-aviation fuel
children.label: ii Non-aviation fuel
children.text: In the case of any transportation fuel which is not a sustainable aviation fuel, the lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions of such fuel shall be based on the most recent determinations under the Greenhouse gases, Regulated Emissions, and Energy use in Transportation model developed by Argonne National Laboratory, or a successor model (as determined by the Secretary).

children.label: I
children.text: the most recent Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation which has been adopted by the International Civil Aviation Organization with the agreement of the United States, or

children.label: II
children.text: any similar methodology which satisfies the criteria under section 211()(1)(H) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)(H)), as in effect on the date of enactment of this section.oo42 U.S.C. 7545

children.heading: Exclusion of indirect land use changes
children.label: iv Exclusion of indirect land use changes
children.text: Notwithstanding clauses (i), (ii), and (iii), the emissions rate shall be adjusted as necessary to exclude any emissions attributed to indirect land use change. Any such adjustment shall be based on regulations or methodologies determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: shall provide a distinct emissions rate with respect to such fuel based on the specific animal manure feedstock, which may include dairy manure, swine manure, poultry manure, or any other sources as are determined appropriate by the Secretary, and

children.label: II
children.text: notwithstanding subparagraph (E), may provide an emissions rate that is less than zero.

children.heading: Rounding of emissions rate
children.label: C Rounding of emissions rate
children.text: The Secretary may round the emissions rates under subparagraph (B) to the nearest multiple of 5 kilograms of CO2e per mmBTU.1So in original. Probably should be “COe”.12

children.heading: Provisional emissions rate
children.label: D Provisional emissions rate
children.text: In the case of any transportation fuel for which an emissions rate has not been established under subparagraph (B), a taxpayer producing such fuel may file a petition with the Secretary for determination of the emissions rate with respect to such fuel.

children.heading: Prohibition on negative emission rates
children.label: E Prohibition on negative emission rates
children.text: For purposes of this section, the emissions rate for a transportation fuel may not be less than zero.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 2 Rounding
children.text: If any amount determined under paragraph (1)(A) is not a multiple of 0.1, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.1.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of calendar years beginning after 2024, the 20 cent amount in subsection (a)(2)(A) and the $1.00 amount in subsection (a)(2)(B) shall each be adjusted by multiplying such amount by the inflation adjustment factor for the calendar year in which the sale of the transportation fuel occurs. If any amount as increased under the preceding sentence is not a multiple of 1 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 1 cent.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment factor
children.label: 2 Inflation adjustment factor
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the inflation adjustment factor shall be the inflation adjustment factor determined and published by the Secretary pursuant to section 45Y(c), determined by substituting “calendar year 2022” for “calendar year 1992” in paragraph (3) thereof.

children.heading: mmBTU
children.label: 1 mmBTU
children.text: The term “mmBTU” means 1,000,000 British thermal units.

children.heading: COe2
children.label: 2 COe2
children.text: The term “COe” means, with respect to any greenhouse gas, the equivalent carbon dioxide (as determined based on relative global warming potential).2

children.heading: Greenhouse gas
children.label: 3 Greenhouse gas
children.text: The term “greenhouse gas” has the same meaning given that term under section 211()(1)(G) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)(G)), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section.oo42 U.S.C. 7545

children.label: A
children.text: means a facility used for the production of transportation fuels, and

children.label: i
children.text: The credit for production of clean hydrogen under section 45V.

children.label: ii
children.text: The credit determined under section 46 to the extent that such credit is attributable to the energy credit determined under section 48 with respect to any specified clean hydrogen production facility for which an election is made under subsection (a)(15) of such section.

children.label: iii
children.text: The credit for carbon oxide sequestration under section 45Q.

children.label: i
children.text: is suitable for use as a fuel in a highway vehicle or aircraft,

children.label: ii
children.text: has an emissions rate which is not greater than 50 kilograms of COe per mmBTU,2

children.label: iii
children.text: is not derived from coprocessing an applicable material (or materials derived from an applicable material) with a feedstock which is not biomass, and

children.label: iv
children.text: is not produced from a fuel for which a credit under this section is allowable.

children.label: I
children.text: monoglycerides, diglycerides, and triglycerides,

children.label: II
children.text: free fatty acids, and

children.label: III
children.text: fatty acid esters.

children.heading: Biomass
children.label: ii Biomass
children.text: The term “biomass” has the same meaning given such term in section 45K(c)(3).

children.heading: Regulations and guidance
children.label: C Regulations and guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of subparagraph (A)(iv).

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: e Guidance
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall issue guidance regarding implementation of this section, including calculation of emissions factors for transportation fuel, the table described in subsection (b)(1)(B)(i), and the determination of clean fuel production credits under this section.January 1, 2025

children.heading: Only registered production in the United States taken into account
children.label: 1 Only registered production in the United States taken into account
I
children.text: is registered as a producer of clean fuel under section 4101 at the time of production, and

children.label: AA
children.text: any general requirements, supply chain traceability requirements, and information transmission requirements established under the Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation described in subclause (I) of subsection (b)(1)(B)(iii), or

children.label: BB
children.text: in the case of any methodology described in subclause (II) of such subsection, requirements similar to the requirements described in subitem (AA), and

children.label: bb
children.text: such other information with respect to such fuel as the Secretary may require for purposes of carrying out this section,

children.label: ii
children.text: such fuel is produced in the United States, and

children.label: iii
children.text: such fuel is exclusively derived from a feedstock which was produced or grown in the United States, Mexico, or Canada.

children.heading: United States
children.label: B United States
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “United States” includes any possession of the United States.

children.heading: Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.label: 2 Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.text: In the case of a facility in which more than 1 person has an ownership interest, except to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, production from the facility shall be allocated among such persons in proportion to their respective ownership interests in the gross sales from such facility.

children.heading: Related persons
children.label: 3 Related persons
children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons would be treated as a single employer under the regulations prescribed under section 52(b). In the case of a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group of corporations filing a consolidated return, such corporation shall be treated as selling fuel to an unrelated person if such fuel is sold to such a person by another member of such group. The Secretary may prescribe additional related person rules similar to the rule described in the preceding sentence for entities which are not described in such sentence, including rules for related persons with respect to which the taxpayer has reason to believe will sell fuel to an unrelated person in a manner described in subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 4 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative
children.label: 5 Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45Y(g)(6) shall apply.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7) shall apply.

children.heading: Special rule for facilities placed in service beforeJanuary 1, 2025
children.label: B Special rule for facilities placed in service beforeJanuary 1, 2025
i
children.text: clause (i) of section 45(b)(7)(A) shall not apply, and

children.label: ii
children.text: clause (ii) of such section shall be applied by substituting “with respect to any taxable year beginning after , for which the credit is allowed under this section” for “with respect to any taxable year, for any portion of such taxable year which is within the period described in subsection (a)(2)(A)(ii)”.December 31, 2024

children.heading: Apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 7 Apprenticeship requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(8) shall apply.

children.heading: Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities
children.label: 8 Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No credit shall be determined under subsection (a) for any taxable year beginning after the date of enactment of this paragraph if the taxpayer is a specified foreign entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(B)).

children.heading: Other prohibited foreign entities
children.label: B Other prohibited foreign entities
children.text: No credit shall be determined under subsection (a) for any taxable year beginning after the date which is 2 years after the date of enactment of this paragraph if the taxpayer is a foreign-influenced entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(D), without regard to clause (i)(II) thereof).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: g Termination
children.text: This section shall not apply to transportation fuel sold after .December 31, 2029
